title: RENAAL
subtitle: Losartan in Type 2 Diabetic Nephropathy — High-Order Why/How Module
authors: Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al.; N Engl J Med 2001

trialDesign.fullName: Reduction of Endpoints in NIDDM with the Angiotensin II Antagonist Losartan (RENAAL)
trialDesign.population: 1513 patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy
trialDesign.duration: Mean follow-up 3.4 years (stopped early; planned 4.5 years)
trialDesign.intervention: Losartan 50 mg daily, titrated to 100 mg if needed
trialDesign.comparator: Placebo; both groups received conventional antihypertensive therapy (no ACE inhibitors)
trialDesign.primaryEndpoint: Composite: doubling serum creatinine, ESRD, or death
trialDesign.overview: Losartan reduced the primary renal composite by 16%, doubling creatinine by 25%, ESRD by 28%, and proteinuria by 35%, with renal protection exceeding blood pressure effects alone.

headings.background: Diabetic nephropathy is the leading cause of ESRD; slowing progression in type 2 diabetes had not been established before RENAAL.
headings.design: Randomized, double-blind; Losartan 50–100 mg vs placebo with conventional antihypertensives; target BP <140/90 mm Hg.
headings.population: Type 2 diabetes with nephropathy: albumin/creatinine ratio ≥300 (or proteinuria ≥0.5 g/day) and creatinine 1.3–3.0 mg/dL.
headings.intervention: Losartan 50 mg → 100 mg if trough BP above target after 4 weeks.
headings.comparator: Placebo plus open-label antihypertensives (diuretics, CCBs, beta-blockers — no ACE inhibitors).
headings.followUp: Mean 3.4 years (early stop due to external ACE-inhibitor CV evidence).

outcomes.label: Primary composite — renal
outcomes.definition: Doubling serum creatinine, ESRD, or death
outcomes.interpretation: 16% reduction (15% after BP adjustment) — exceeds BP effect alone

outcomes.label: First heart failure hospitalization
outcomes.interpretation: Significant despite neutral overall CV composite

safetyHighlights: Discontinuation for adverse events: 17.2% losartan vs 21.7% placebo
Hyperkalemia/creatinine-related discontinuation rare but slightly higher with losartan
trialSummary.clinicalBottomLine: Losartan provides global renal protection in type 2 diabetic nephropathy beyond blood pressure lowering, with meaningful ESRD delay and reduced heart failure hospitalization.

inclusionCriteria: Type 2 diabetes with nephropathy: urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio ≥300 on two occasions (or proteinuria ≥0.5 g/day)
Serum creatinine 1.3–3.0 mg/dL (men >60 kg: lower limit 1.5 mg/dL)
Target blood pressure <140/90 mm Hg with conventional therapy (ACE inhibitors/ARBs discontinued at screening)
exclusionCriteria: Myocardial infarction or CABG within previous month
ACE inhibitor or angiotensin-II-receptor antagonist use (discontinued before randomization)
keyTakeaways: Primary renal composite reduced 16%; doubling creatinine ↓25%; ESRD ↓28%; proteinuria ↓35%.
Renal benefit persisted after adjusting for blood pressure — not BP control alone.
No overall CV morbidity/mortality benefit, but first HF hospitalization ↓32%.
Mean follow-up 3.4 years after early termination; losartan well tolerated vs placebo.

items.title: RENAAL — Main Points (High-Yield Overview)
items.content:
Background and Rationale: Diabetic nephropathy is the leading cause of end-stage renal disease, but prior to RENAAL, data on slowing its progression in type 2 diabetes were lacking.

Study Design: Randomized, double-blind trial in 1513 patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy — Losartan 50–100 mg daily vs placebo, both with conventional antihypertensive therapy (excluding ACE inhibitors and ARBs other than study drug).

Primary Renal Outcomes: Losartan reduced the primary composite (doubling serum creatinine, ESRD, or death) by 16%; doubling creatinine ↓25%; ESRD ↓28%; proteinuria ↓35%.

Cardiovascular Outcomes: Composite CV morbidity/mortality was similar; first heart failure hospitalization ↓32%.

Safety: Renal protection exceeded attributable blood pressure change; tolerability similar to placebo.
keyPoints: First major T2DM nephropathy trial showing ARB slows ESRD
16% composite renal ↓ · 28% ESRD ↓ · 35% proteinuria ↓
HF hospitalization ↓32% without overall CV composite benefit
items.reference: Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why was the RENAAL study specifically initiated for patients with type 2 diabetes?
items.answer: While interrupting the renin-angiotensin system was known to slow renal disease in type 1 diabetes, postponing end-stage renal disease in type 2 diabetes—the most common form of the disease—remained an elusive goal.
keyPoints: While interrupting the renin-angiotensin system was known to slow renal disease in type 1 diabetes, postponing end-stage renal disease in type 2 diabetes—the most common form of the disease—remained an elusive goal.
items.reference: RENAAL Q1: "While interrupting the renin-angiotensin system was known to slow renal disease in type 1 diabetes, postponing end-stage..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How was baseline nephropathy formally defined for patient inclusion?
items.answer: Nephropathy was defined by two occurrences of a urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio of at least 300 (or urinary protein excretion of ≥0.5 g per day) alongside a serum creatinine value between 1.3 and 3.0 mg per deciliter.
keyPoints: Nephropathy was defined by two occurrences of a urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio of at least 300 (or urinary protein excretion of ≥0.5 g per day) alongside a serum creatinine value between 1.3 and 3.0 mg per deciliter.
items.reference: RENAAL Q2: "Nephropathy was defined by two occurrences of a urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio of at least 300 (or urinary protein ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why was the study prematurely terminated by the steering committee?
items.answer: The study was stopped early due to new external evidence suggesting that ACE inhibitors (which were explicitly excluded from this trial) might effectively reduce cardiovascular events in patients with diabetic renal impairment.
keyPoints: The study was stopped early due to new external evidence suggesting that ACE inhibitors (which were explicitly excluded from this trial) might effectively reduce cardiovascular events in patients with diabetic renal impairment.
items.reference: RENAAL Q3: "The study was stopped early due to new external evidence suggesting that ACE inhibitors (which were explicitly excluded ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the early termination affect the study's timeline?
items.answer: The planned 4.5-year mean follow-up time was cut short, resulting in an actual mean follow-up time of 3.4 years.
keyPoints: The planned 4.5-year mean follow-up time was cut short, resulting in an actual mean follow-up time of 3.4 years.
items.reference: RENAAL Q4: "The planned 4.5-year mean follow-up time was cut short, resulting in an actual mean follow-up time of 3.4 years." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How were patients already taking ACE inhibitors or angiotensin-II-receptor antagonists managed during screening?
items.answer: These medications were discontinued and replaced with alternative open-label medications, such as diuretics, calcium-channel antagonists, or beta-blockers.
keyPoints: These medications were discontinued and replaced with alternative open-label medications, such as diuretics, calcium-channel antagonists, or beta-blockers.
items.reference: RENAAL Q5: "These medications were discontinued and replaced with alternative open-label medications, such as diuretics, calcium-cha..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How was the dosing of Losartan managed throughout the study?
items.answer: Patients started on 50 mg of Losartan once daily; after four weeks, the dose was increased to 100 mg if the patient's trough blood pressure remained above the target level.
keyPoints: Patients started on 50 mg of Losartan once daily; after four weeks, the dose was increased to 100 mg if the patient's trough blood pressure remained above the target level.
items.reference: RENAAL Q6: "Patients started on 50 mg of Losartan once daily; after four weeks, the dose was increased to 100 mg if the patient's tr..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the trial define its target blood pressure for patients?
items.answer: The target was defined as a systolic blood pressure of less than 140 mm Hg and a diastolic blood pressure of less than 90 mm Hg.
keyPoints: The target was defined as a systolic blood pressure of less than 140 mm Hg and a diastolic blood pressure of less than 90 mm Hg.
items.reference: RENAAL Q7: "The target was defined as a systolic blood pressure of less than 140 mm Hg and a diastolic blood pressure of less than 9..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why were patients with recent myocardial infarctions or strokes excluded?
items.answer: Patients were excluded if they had a myocardial infarction or coronary artery bypass within the previous month, or a stroke within the previous six months, to isolate renal efficacy and avoid acute, severe cardiovascular baseline confounders.
keyPoints: Patients were excluded if they had a myocardial infarction or coronary artery bypass within the previous month, or a stroke within the previous six months, to isolate renal efficacy and avoid acute, severe cardiovascular baseline confounders.
items.reference: RENAAL Q8: "Patients were excluded if they had a myocardial infarction or coronary artery bypass within the previous month, or a str..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How was a "doubling of the serum creatinine concentration" rigorously verified?
items.answer: It was defined as the first serum creatinine value that was twice the baseline value, confirmed by a second measurement obtained at least four weeks after the initial spike.
keyPoints: It was defined as the first serum creatinine value that was twice the baseline value, confirmed by a second measurement obtained at least four weeks after the initial spike.
items.reference: RENAAL Q9: "It was defined as the first serum creatinine value that was twice the baseline value, confirmed by a second measurement ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How was end-stage renal disease defined in the context of the primary endpoint?
items.answer: End-stage renal disease was defined by the clinical need for long-term dialysis or renal transplantation.
keyPoints: End-stage renal disease was defined by the clinical need for long-term dialysis or renal transplantation.
items.reference: RENAAL Q10: "End-stage renal disease was defined by the clinical need for long-term dialysis or renal transplantation." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why were data from patients who died censored in specific analyses?
items.answer: In analyses of nonfatal endpoints, data for patients who died were censored to prevent mortality from confounding the statistical measurement of nonfatal occurrences.
keyPoints: In analyses of nonfatal endpoints, data for patients who died were censored to prevent mortality from confounding the statistical measurement of nonfatal occurrences.
items.reference: RENAAL Q11: "In analyses of nonfatal endpoints, data for patients who died were censored to prevent mortality from confounding the st..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the statistical analysis account for baseline proteinuria differences?
items.answer: The study used a Cox regression model that explicitly included the baseline level of proteinuria as a stratification factor.
keyPoints: The study used a Cox regression model that explicitly included the baseline level of proteinuria as a stratification factor.
items.reference: RENAAL Q12: "The study used a Cox regression model that explicitly included the baseline level of proteinuria as a stratification fac..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the researchers check if blood pressure differences alone drove the results?
items.answer: They added mean arterial pressure during treatment as a time-dependent covariate in the Cox model and compared the estimated effect of Losartan against the primary analysis.
keyPoints: They added mean arterial pressure during treatment as a time-dependent covariate in the Cox model and compared the estimated effect of Losartan against the primary analysis.
items.reference: RENAAL Q13: "They added mean arterial pressure during treatment as a time-dependent covariate in the Cox model and compared the estim..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How was the progression of renal disease mathematically tracked?
items.answer: Renal disease progression was assessed by comparing the slopes of the reciprocal of the serum creatinine concentration using a linear random-effects model.
keyPoints: Renal disease progression was assessed by comparing the slopes of the reciprocal of the serum creatinine concentration using a linear random-effects model.
items.reference: RENAAL Q14: "Renal disease progression was assessed by comparing the slopes of the reciprocal of the serum creatinine concentration u..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why was an intention-to-treat approach the primary method of statistical analysis?
items.answer: It ensured that data from all randomized patients (except three lost to follow-up) were included from randomization to termination, preserving the integrity of the randomized groups regardless of treatment adherence.
keyPoints: It ensured that data from all randomized patients (except three lost to follow-up) were included from randomization to termination, preserving the integrity of the randomized groups regardless of treatment adherence.
items.reference: RENAAL Q15: "It ensured that data from all randomized patients (except three lost to follow-up) were included from randomization to t..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the per-protocol analysis differ from the intention-to-treat analysis?
items.answer: The per-protocol analysis excluded patients who violated inclusion/exclusion criteria and censored patient data 14 days after they permanently discontinued study medication.
keyPoints: The per-protocol analysis excluded patients who violated inclusion/exclusion criteria and censored patient data 14 days after they permanently discontinued study medication.
items.reference: RENAAL Q16: "The per-protocol analysis excluded patients who violated inclusion/exclusion criteria and censored patient data 14 days ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did Losartan affect the primary composite endpoint?
items.answer: Losartan reduced the overall risk of the primary composite endpoint (doubling of serum creatinine, end-stage renal disease, or death) by 16% compared with placebo.
keyPoints: Losartan reduced the overall risk of the primary composite endpoint (doubling of serum creatinine, end-stage renal disease, or death) by 16% compared with placebo.
items.reference: RENAAL Q17: "Losartan reduced the overall risk of the primary composite endpoint (doubling of serum creatinine, end-stage renal disea..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why did the primary endpoint risk reduction slightly alter after correcting for blood pressure?
items.answer: Because there was a small, time-averaged difference in blood pressure between groups, adjusting for this changed the risk reduction from 16% to 15%, proving renal benefits exceeded simple blood pressure control.
keyPoints: Because there was a small, time-averaged difference in blood pressure between groups, adjusting for this changed the risk reduction from 16% to 15%, proving renal benefits exceeded simple blood pressure control.
items.reference: RENAAL Q18: "Because there was a small, time-averaged difference in blood pressure between groups, adjusting for this changed the ris..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did Losartan impact the specific risk of doubling serum creatinine?
items.answer: The risk of doubling the serum creatinine concentration was 25% lower in the Losartan group than in the placebo group.
keyPoints: The risk of doubling the serum creatinine concentration was 25% lower in the Losartan group than in the placebo group.
items.reference: RENAAL Q19: "The risk of doubling the serum creatinine concentration was 25% lower in the Losartan group than in the placebo group." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did Losartan impact the incidence of end-stage renal disease?
items.answer: Treatment with Losartan reduced the risk of end-stage renal disease by 28%.
keyPoints: Treatment with Losartan reduced the risk of end-stage renal disease by 28%.
items.reference: RENAAL Q20: "Treatment with Losartan reduced the risk of end-stage renal disease by 28%." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why did Losartan have no significant impact on overall mortality?
items.answer: Approximately 20% of patients died in both groups, yielding no significant difference (P=0.88), indicating renal protection without reduced all-cause mortality over 3.4 years.
keyPoints: Approximately 20% of patients died in both groups, yielding no significant difference (P=0.88), indicating renal protection without reduced all-cause mortality over 3.4 years.
items.reference: RENAAL Q21: "Approximately 20% of patients died in both groups, yielding no significant difference (P=0.88), indicating renal protect..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did Losartan alter the composite of cardiovascular morbidity and mortality?
items.answer: There was no significant difference between groups; roughly one-third of patients in both Losartan (32.9%) and placebo (35.2%) groups experienced a fatal or nonfatal cardiovascular event.
keyPoints: There was no significant difference between groups; roughly one-third of patients in both Losartan (32.9%) and placebo (35.2%) groups experienced a fatal or nonfatal cardiovascular event.
items.reference: RENAAL Q22: "There was no significant difference between groups; roughly one-third of patients in both Losartan (32.9%) and placebo (..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why was the finding regarding heart failure hospitalization significant?
items.answer: Despite no overall cardiovascular composite benefit, Losartan significantly reduced the rate of first hospitalization for heart failure by 32%.
keyPoints: Despite no overall cardiovascular composite benefit, Losartan significantly reduced the rate of first hospitalization for heart failure by 32%.
items.reference: RENAAL Q23: "Despite no overall cardiovascular composite benefit, Losartan significantly reduced the rate of first hospitalization fo..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did preexisting heart failure impact the heart failure hospitalization data?
items.answer: When the 88 patients with preexisting heart failure at randomization were excluded, a significant difference favoring Losartan in first heart failure hospitalizations remained.
keyPoints: When the 88 patients with preexisting heart failure at randomization were excluded, a significant difference favoring Losartan in first heart failure hospitalizations remained.
items.reference: RENAAL Q24: "When the 88 patients with preexisting heart failure at randomization were excluded, a significant difference favoring Lo..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the drug influence myocardial infarction rates?
items.answer: There was a 28% relative risk reduction in myocardial infarction in the Losartan group (6.7%) versus placebo (8.9%), but this did not reach statistical significance.
keyPoints: There was a 28% relative risk reduction in myocardial infarction in the Losartan group (6.7%) versus placebo (8.9%), but this did not reach statistical significance.
items.reference: RENAAL Q25: "There was a 28% relative risk reduction in myocardial infarction in the Losartan group (6.7%) versus placebo (8.9%), but..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did Losartan alter patient proteinuria profiles over the trial?
items.answer: Losartan led to an average 35% reduction in proteinuria, whereas proteinuria in the placebo group tended to increase.
keyPoints: Losartan led to an average 35% reduction in proteinuria, whereas proteinuria in the placebo group tended to increase.
items.reference: RENAAL Q26: "Losartan led to an average 35% reduction in proteinuria, whereas proteinuria in the placebo group tended to increase." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did Losartan impact the estimated decline in glomerular filtration rate (GFR)?
items.answer: Losartan was associated with a 15.2% reduction in the estimated decline in GFR compared with placebo.
keyPoints: Losartan was associated with a 15.2% reduction in the estimated decline in GFR compared with placebo.
items.reference: RENAAL Q27: "Losartan was associated with a 15.2% reduction in the estimated decline in GFR compared with placebo." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why did more patients in the placebo group discontinue study treatment?
items.answer: Overall, 53.5% of placebo patients discontinued treatment compared with 46.5% of Losartan patients, with adverse clinical events driving 21.7% of placebo dropouts versus 17.2% for Losartan.
keyPoints: Overall, 53.5% of placebo patients discontinued treatment compared with 46.5% of Losartan patients, with adverse clinical events driving 21.7% of placebo dropouts versus 17.2% for Losartan.
items.reference: RENAAL Q28: "Overall, 53.5% of placebo patients discontinued treatment compared with 46.5% of Losartan patients, with adverse clinica..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How frequently did hyperkalemia or increased creatinine force medication discontinuation?
items.answer: Discontinuation due to increased creatinine or potassium was rare but slightly higher in the Losartan group (1.5% and 1.1%, respectively) versus placebo (1.2% and 0.5%).
keyPoints: Discontinuation due to increased creatinine or potassium was rare but slightly higher in the Losartan group (1.5% and 1.1%, respectively) versus placebo (1.2% and 0.5%).
items.reference: RENAAL Q29: "Discontinuation due to increased creatinine or potassium was rare but slightly higher in the Losartan group (1.5% and 1...." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did baseline blood pressure characteristics compare between the two groups?
items.answer: At baseline, trough blood pressure was nearly identical, averaging 152/82 mm Hg in the Losartan group and 153/82 mm Hg in the placebo group.
keyPoints: At baseline, trough blood pressure was nearly identical, averaging 152/82 mm Hg in the Losartan group and 153/82 mm Hg in the placebo group.
items.reference: RENAAL Q30: "At baseline, trough blood pressure was nearly identical, averaging 152/82 mm Hg in the Losartan group and 153/82 mm Hg i..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did blood pressure change for both groups after one year of treatment?
items.answer: Blood pressure dropped in both groups, averaging 146/78 mm Hg in the Losartan group and 150/80 mm Hg in the placebo group.
keyPoints: Blood pressure dropped in both groups, averaging 146/78 mm Hg in the Losartan group and 150/80 mm Hg in the placebo group.
items.reference: RENAAL Q31: "Blood pressure dropped in both groups, averaging 146/78 mm Hg in the Losartan group and 150/80 mm Hg in the placebo grou..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why do the authors conclude that Losartan provides "global renal protection"?
items.answer: This follows from combined evidence of reduced doubling of serum creatinine, delayed ESRD, smaller slopes of reciprocal serum creatinine, and lowered proteinuria.
keyPoints: This follows from combined evidence of reduced doubling of serum creatinine, delayed ESRD, smaller slopes of reciprocal serum creatinine, and lowered proteinuria.
items.reference: RENAAL Q32: "This follows from combined evidence of reduced doubling of serum creatinine, delayed ESRD, smaller slopes of reciprocal ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How does this study contrast with previous studies using ACE inhibitors for diabetic nephropathy?
items.answer: Prior studies showed ACE inhibitors improved proteinuria but failed to clearly demonstrate superiority in slowing progression to ESRD over non-blockade therapies in type 2 diabetes.
keyPoints: Prior studies showed ACE inhibitors improved proteinuria but failed to clearly demonstrate superiority in slowing progression to ESRD over non-blockade therapies in type 2 diabetes.
items.reference: RENAAL Q33: "Prior studies showed ACE inhibitors improved proteinuria but failed to clearly demonstrate superiority in slowing progre..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why was it considered safe to allow concurrent calcium-channel antagonist therapy?
items.answer: Simultaneous calcium-channel antagonist therapy did not detract from Losartan's beneficial effects, despite theoretical controversies about renal protection with these agents.
keyPoints: Simultaneous calcium-channel antagonist therapy did not detract from Losartan's beneficial effects, despite theoretical controversies about renal protection with these agents.
items.reference: RENAAL Q34: "Simultaneous calcium-channel antagonist therapy did not detract from Losartan's beneficial effects, despite theoretical ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How might Losartan counteract a specific negative effect of calcium-channel antagonists?
items.answer: Calcium-channel antagonists can augment angiotensin II production; concomitant angiotensin-II-receptor blockade with Losartan may counteract this response.
keyPoints: Calcium-channel antagonists can augment angiotensin II production; concomitant angiotensin-II-receptor blockade with Losartan may counteract this response.
items.reference: RENAAL Q35: "Calcium-channel antagonists can augment angiotensin II production; concomitant angiotensin-II-receptor blockade with Los..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why might the strict enrollment criteria have impacted the cardiovascular morbidity results?
items.answer: Exclusion of patients at high cardiovascular risk (including recent MI or stroke) limited the at-risk sample, potentially contributing to the lack of significant overall cardiovascular outcome difference.
keyPoints: Exclusion of patients at high cardiovascular risk (including recent MI or stroke) limited the at-risk sample, potentially contributing to the lack of significant overall cardiovascular outcome difference.
items.reference: RENAAL Q36: "Exclusion of patients at high cardiovascular risk (including recent MI or stroke) limited the at-risk sample, potentiall..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How do the heart failure findings in RENAAL contrast with the HOPE study?
items.answer: HOPE showed ACE inhibitors improved heart failure signs but not significant hospitalization reduction; RENAAL showed angiotensin II blockade decreased risk of overt heart failure requiring hospitalization.
keyPoints: HOPE showed ACE inhibitors improved heart failure signs but not significant hospitalization reduction; RENAAL showed angiotensin II blockade decreased risk of overt heart failure requiring hospitalization.
items.reference: RENAAL Q37: "HOPE showed ACE inhibitors improved heart failure signs but not significant hospitalization reduction; RENAAL showed ang..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why is the clinical extrapolability of the 28% ESRD reduction considered highly significant?
items.answer: The authors extrapolated that the 28% reduction corresponds to an average delay of two years in the need for dialysis or renal transplantation.
keyPoints: The authors extrapolated that the 28% reduction corresponds to an average delay of two years in the need for dialysis or renal transplantation.
items.reference: RENAAL Q38: "The authors extrapolated that the 28% reduction corresponds to an average delay of two years in the need for dialysis or..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How was the safety profile of Losartan assessed relative to conventional therapy?
items.answer: Losartan demonstrated excellent tolerability, with adverse-event discontinuation rates similar to and slightly lower than placebo on standard care.
keyPoints: Losartan demonstrated excellent tolerability, with adverse-event discontinuation rates similar to and slightly lower than placebo on standard care.
items.reference: RENAAL Q39: "Losartan demonstrated excellent tolerability, with adverse-event discontinuation rates similar to and slightly lower tha..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why is the prevention of diabetic nephropathy framed as a pressing economic issue?
items.answer: ESRD costs in the US reached $12 billion in 1998 and were projected to surpass $28 billion by 2010, making prevention essential to reduce financial strain.
keyPoints: ESRD costs in the US reached $12 billion in 1998 and were projected to surpass $28 billion by 2010, making prevention essential to reduce financial strain.
items.reference: RENAAL Q40: "ESRD costs in the US reached $12 billion in 1998 and were projected to surpass $28 billion by 2010, making prevention es..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How heavily did diabetes contribute to the overall burden of end-stage renal disease during the 1990s?
items.answer: Per the NIH, diabetes was the single largest cause, accounting for approximately 40% of all US ESRD cases between 1994 and 1998.
keyPoints: Per the NIH, diabetes was the single largest cause, accounting for approximately 40% of all US ESRD cases between 1994 and 1998.
items.reference: RENAAL Q41: "Per the NIH, diabetes was the single largest cause, accounting for approximately 40% of all US ESRD cases between 1994 a..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How was missing dialysis or death data collected for patients who could not attend clinic visits?
items.answer: If a patient could not return to clinic, the patient or a family member was contacted by telephone to determine whether ESRD endpoints or death had occurred.
keyPoints: If a patient could not return to clinic, the patient or a family member was contacted by telephone to determine whether ESRD endpoints or death had occurred.
items.reference: RENAAL Q42: "If a patient could not return to clinic, the patient or a family member was contacted by telephone to determine whether ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why were beta-blockers and diuretics frequently used in both the Losartan and placebo groups?
items.answer: The protocol required adding standard antihypertensive agents (excluding ACE inhibitors and angiotensin-II antagonists) to achieve target blood pressure below 140/90 mm Hg.
keyPoints: The protocol required adding standard antihypertensive agents (excluding ACE inhibitors and angiotensin-II antagonists) to achieve target blood pressure below 140/90 mm Hg.
items.reference: RENAAL Q43: "The protocol required adding standard antihypertensive agents (excluding ACE inhibitors and angiotensin-II antagonists) ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the geographic distribution of patients influence the statistical modeling?
items.answer: The Cox regression model included geographic region as a covariate so regional variations in care or genetics would not skew primary outcomes.
keyPoints: The Cox regression model included geographic region as a covariate so regional variations in care or genetics would not skew primary outcomes.
items.reference: RENAAL Q44: "The Cox regression model included geographic region as a covariate so regional variations in care or genetics would not ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why was an alpha spending function of the O'Brien-Fleming type necessary?
items.answer: Because the steering committee conducted interim analyses, this boundary required a critical P value of 0.048 for the primary hypothesis to maintain statistical rigor.
keyPoints: Because the steering committee conducted interim analyses, this boundary required a critical P value of 0.048 for the primary hypothesis to maintain statistical rigor.
items.reference: RENAAL Q45: "Because the steering committee conducted interim analyses, this boundary required a critical P value of 0.048 for the pr..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the per-protocol risk reduction for the primary endpoint compare to the intention-to-treat analysis?
items.answer: Per-protocol analysis showed a 22% risk reduction (P=0.008) versus 16% in the intention-to-treat analysis.
keyPoints: Per-protocol analysis showed a 22% risk reduction (P=0.008) versus 16% in the intention-to-treat analysis.
items.reference: RENAAL Q46: "Per-protocol analysis showed a 22% risk reduction (P=0.008) versus 16% in the intention-to-treat analysis." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why was a lower limit of serum creatinine at 1.5 mg/dl used for certain men during screening?
items.answer: This lower limit applied to male patients weighing more than 60 kg to accurately capture baseline nephropathy relative to larger body mass.
keyPoints: This lower limit applied to male patients weighing more than 60 kg to accurately capture baseline nephropathy relative to larger body mass.
items.reference: RENAAL Q47: "This lower limit applied to male patients weighing more than 60 kg to accurately capture baseline nephropathy relative t..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How many total patients withdrew their consent to participate in the study?
items.answer: Consent was withdrawn by 7.5% of patients in the Losartan group and 7.8% in the placebo group.
keyPoints: Consent was withdrawn by 7.5% of patients in the Losartan group and 7.8% in the placebo group.
items.reference: RENAAL Q48: "Consent was withdrawn by 7.5% of patients in the Losartan group and 7.8% in the placebo group." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: Why is the reduction in GFR decline noted as "far smaller" than previous type 1 diabetes studies?
items.answer: The 15.2% Losartan GFR-decline reduction was smaller than reductions reported nearly a decade earlier with captopril in type 1 diabetes, highlighting differing pathological aggressiveness between type 1 and type 2 disease.
keyPoints: The 15.2% Losartan GFR-decline reduction was smaller than reductions reported nearly a decade earlier with captopril in type 1 diabetes, highlighting differing pathological aggressiveness between type 1 and type 2 disease.
items.reference: RENAAL Q49: "The 15.2% Losartan GFR-decline reduction was smaller than reductions reported nearly a decade earlier with captopril in ..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.

items.question: How did the overall rate of conventional antihypertensive usage change from baseline to study treatment?
items.answer: At baseline, over 92% used conventional therapies; during the study, use of diuretics, alpha-blockers, beta-blockers, and calcium-channel antagonists increased in both groups to reach blood pressure targets.
keyPoints: At baseline, over 92% used conventional therapies; during the study, use of diuretics, alpha-blockers, beta-blockers, and calcium-channel antagonists increased in both groups to reach blood pressure targets.
items.reference: RENAAL Q50: "At baseline, over 92% used conventional therapies; during the study, use of diuretics, alpha-blockers, beta-blockers, an..." — Brenner BM, Cooper ME, de Zeeuw D, et al. Effects of losartan on renal and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and nephropathy. N Engl J Med 2001;345:861-869.
